ownerTitle: Business Administration: Supply Chain Management (BA) - Management Concentration
primarySourceLabel: UW Bothell Supply Chain Management option major requirements
sourceLabel: UW Bothell Supply Chain Management option major requirements
requirementCueLines: (*Note: Students in the process of completing a course will not be considered for the Supply Chain Management Option until the course has been completed. Talk with an advisor for more information).
10 credits – 2 courses from the list below:
BBUS 300 – Organizational Behavior, Ethics and Inclusivity, Diversity Designated Course
BBUS 307 – Business Writing, Writing Designated Course
BBUS 310 – Managerial Economics (BMATH 144, STMATH 124, or MATH 112)
BBUS 447 – Retail Operations & Supply Chain Management (300)
BBUS 482 – Introduction to Supply Chain Management (340)
BBUS 487 – Management and Improving Supply Chain Processes (441, 482)
BBUS 492 – Supply Chain Field Study (441, 482, 483, 486, 487) , or BBUS 497 – Internship, or BBUS 499 – Independent Research, or BBUS 491 – Business Consulting
Before you can register for the capstone courses, you must complete all core courses and meet with your advisor to file a graduation application .
Students must complete the following courses for graduation:
